questions.question: Which of the following sentences correctly uses the idiom "**A picture is worth a thousand words**"?
options: I prefer reading detailed descriptions rather than looking at images, even though a picture is worth a thousand words.
The beautiful photograph captured the tragedy so well that no words were needed-truly, a picture is worth a thousand words.
Since she couldn't take a picture, she decided to write a long paragraph, as a picture is worth a thousand words.
He described the entire scene in detail because a picture is worth a thousand words.
questions.answer: The beautiful photograph captured the tragedy so well that no words were needed-truly, a picture is worth a thousand words.
questions.explanation: Option A: This option states: I prefer reading detailed descriptions rather than looking at images, even though a picture is worth a thousand words. Option B: This option states: The beautiful photograph captured the tragedy so well that no words were needed-truly, a picture is worth a thousand words. Option C: This option states: Since she couldn't take a picture, she decided to write a long paragraph, as a picture is worth a thousand words. Option D: This option states: He described the entire scene in detail because a picture is worth a thousand words. **Option B** correctly applies: this option gives the accepted meaning/usage of the idiom or phrase in the question.
